# Multi Tenant Organization

> Multi-tenant organization model with isolated data, user management, and role-based access control

**Category:** Infrastructure · **Version:** 1.0.0 · **Tags:** multi-tenant · organization · company · isolation · rbac · users

## What this does

Multi-tenant organization model with isolated data, user management, and role-based access control

Specifies 5 acceptance outcomes that any implementation must satisfy, regardless of language or framework.

## Fields

- **org_id** *(text, required)* — Organization ID
- **name** *(text, required)* — Organization Name
- **slug** *(text, required)* — URL Slug
- **owner_uuid** *(text, required)* — Owner
- **description** *(text, optional)* — Description
- **website_url** *(url, optional)* — Website
- **phone** *(phone, optional)* — Phone
- **currency** *(text, optional)* — Default Currency
- **country** *(text, optional)* — Country
- **timezone** *(text, optional)* — Timezone
- **plan** *(text, optional)* — Subscription Plan
- **options** *(json, optional)* — Feature Flags
- **status** *(select, required)* — Status
- **type** *(text, optional)* — Organization Type

## What must be true

- **company_uuid_required:** Each record (order, driver, vehicle, etc.) must be associated with exactly one organization via company_uuid
- **no_cross_tenant:** API requests must always include a valid organization context; cross-tenant access is forbidden
- **unique_slug:** Organization slugs must be globally unique and URL-safe
- **owner_immutable:** The organization owner always has full access and cannot be removed
- **membership_tracking:** User membership is tracked via a company_users join record
- **rbac:** Role-based access control (RBAC) enforces per-resource permissions within the organization
- **suspended_no_api:** Suspended organizations lose API access and cannot create new records
- **data_isolation:** Data for one organization must never appear in API responses for another
- **invite_based_onboarding:** Invitation-based member onboarding requires an invite link with expiry
- **timezone_usage:** Timezone settings are used for all date/time displays and scheduled operations

## Success & failure scenarios

**✅ Success paths**

- **Org Created** — when name exists; owner_uuid exists, then Organization created with owner as first admin member.
- **Member Invited** — when inviting user is admin of the organization; email provided, then Invitation sent to email address.
- **Member Joined** — when valid invite link accepted by user, then User added as a member of the organization.
- **Org Suspended** — when account policy violation or payment failure detected, then Organization suspended; all API access revoked.

**❌ Failure paths**

- **Cross Tenant Access Blocked** — when request references a resource belonging to a different organization, then Access denied — resource not found in your organization. *(error: `ORG_CROSS_TENANT_ACCESS`)*

## Errors it can return

- `ORG_CROSS_TENANT_ACCESS` — The requested resource was not found.
- `ORG_SUSPENDED` — Your organization account is currently suspended. Please contact support.
- `ORG_DUPLICATE_SLUG` — An organization with this name already exists.
- `ORG_INVITE_EXPIRED` — This invitation link has expired or has already been used.

## Connects to

- **fleet-public-api** *(required)* — API authentication is scoped to organization credentials
- **order-lifecycle-webhooks** *(required)* — Webhooks are isolated per organization
- **fleet-customer-contacts** *(recommended)* — Contacts are scoped to the organization
